long_tail: how much overlap between SOC 2 and HIPAA controls
keyword.serp_notes: Current top results are generic framework comparison pages from Vanta and Drata. No one has published a detailed control-level crosswalk with practical mapping guidance. Opportunity to own this with depth.

audience.reader_context: Managing or about to manage both SOC 2 and HIPAA. Suspects there's significant overlap but can't quantify it or operationalize it. May be duplicating control testing across frameworks. Likely in healthcare, healthtech, or a SaaS company handling PHI.
audience.reader_sophistication: high — knows both frameworks but hasn't done formal mapping
audience.reader_intent: Wants to understand the specific overlap so they can reduce duplicated compliance effort. Secondarily, wants validation that multi-framework management should be unified.

angle.headline: The 40% You're Testing Twice: Mapping SOC 2 and HIPAA Control Overlap
angle.thesis: Most organizations managing SOC 2 and HIPAA treat them as separate compliance programs — and end up testing 35-45% of their controls twice. A structured crosswalk between SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria and HIPAA Security Rule requirements reveals which controls satisfy both, eliminating hundreds of hours of duplicated audit work.
angle.hook: A VP of Compliance at a 2,000-person healthcare company told us: 'We estimated that forty percent of our SOC 2 controls overlap with HIPAA requirements. That's hundreds of controls we're testing twice.' He's not wrong — and the duplication gets worse when you add ISO 27001.
angle.differentiation: Competitors publish generic 'SOC 2 vs HIPAA' comparison pages. We'll publish the actual control-level crosswalk with specific mapping (e.g., CC6.1 → 164.312(d)) and practical guidance on how to unify testing.

sections.heading: Why SOC 2 and HIPAA Overlap More Than You Think
sections.purpose: Establish the problem and quantify the overlap (35-45% based on our data). Explain why most organizations miss it.
key_points: Both frameworks address access controls, monitoring, incident response, and risk management
Overlap is obscured because the frameworks use different terminology and structure
SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria vs. HIPAA Security Rule administrative/technical/physical safeguards — different taxonomy, same operational controls

sections.heading: The SOC 2 to HIPAA Control Crosswalk
sections.purpose: Provide the specific mapping between SOC 2 TSC and HIPAA Security Rule requirements. This is the differentiated asset no competitor has published at this depth.
key_points: CC6.1 (Logical and physical access controls) → 164.312(d) (Person or entity authentication)
Include full mapping table (8-10 key overlaps) with practical notes

sections.heading: How to Unify Your Testing Without Cutting Corners
sections.purpose: Practical guidance on how to consolidate control testing across frameworks. Address the fear that unified testing means weaker evidence.
key_points: Map controls to a single internal control library with framework tags
Design evidence collection to satisfy the stricter requirement (usually HIPAA for healthcare-specific controls)
Maintain framework-specific views for auditors who only need to see their scope
Common mistake: testing at the lowest common denominator instead of the highest common bar

sections.heading: What Changes When You Add ISO 27001
sections.purpose: Extend the crosswalk to ISO 27001 for readers considering a third framework. Show how overlap increases with each framework added (argument for unified approach).
key_points: ISO 27001 Annex A controls overlap with both SOC 2 and HIPAA substantially
Three-way overlap can reach 55-65% of total controls
The case for unified compliance grows stronger with each framework
Practical threshold: at 2+ frameworks, tool-based control mapping pays for itself in the first audit cycle

value_props_to_weave.weave_point: Section 3 — when discussing how to unify testing, reference that manual crosswalks in spreadsheets are fragile and time-consuming. A platform with native mapping automates this.

Section 3 — when discussing evidence that satisfies multiple frameworks, note that manual evidence collection multiplied across frameworks is the real time sink. Automation addresses this at the collection layer.
value_props_to_weave.touch: light — workflow reality, not feature pitch

value_props_to_weave.weave_point: Section 3 — when addressing auditor needs for scoped views, mention that unified internal management doesn't mean auditors see everything. Scoped views give each auditor exactly their framework's evidence.

originality_nuggets.nugget: Customer quote from MeridianCare discovery call about 40% overlap and testing twice
Three-way overlap percentage (55-65%) when adding ISO 27001
originality_nuggets.usage: Section 4 — forward-looking value of unified approach

internal_links.context: Section 1 — for readers exploring the broader multi-framework challenge

internal_links.anchor_text: case study: how a 500-person healthcare company reduced audit prep by 60%
internal_links.context: Section 3 — proof point for unified approach outcomes
Section 4 — for readers about to add ISO 27001

seo.title_tag: SOC 2 and HIPAA Control Overlap: The Crosswalk Guide | ComplianceOS
seo.meta_description: 35-45% of SOC 2 and HIPAA controls overlap. This guide maps the specific crosswalk between Trust Services Criteria and HIPAA Security Rule requirements — so you stop testing controls twice.
